steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researchers found that great blue herons prefer deep water during breeding season, but small dark herons do not care about water depth. The conclusion must logically follow from this asymmetry: water-depth management strategies should affect the two species differently.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "attract great blue herons to the area than they are to attract small dark herons to the area.": water-depth strategies would more effectively target great blue herons (depth-sensitive) than small dark herons (depth-insensitive). Follows directly from the finding. ✓
  • "decrease the area's appeal to both great blue herons and small dark herons than they are to increase the appeal to either.": contradicts the purpose of water management and the finding about great blue herons preferring deep water. ✗
  • "extend the average lifespan of great blue herons already in the area than they are to attract a greater number of those birds to the area.": the study is about habitat selection, not lifespan. Introduces an out-of-scope claim. ✗
  • "attract birds that don't typically live in wetlands to the area than they are to attract great blue herons or small dark herons to the area.": the study only concerns these two wetland species; non-wetland birds are never discussed. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
